GET /accounts/{account_id}/vuln_scanner/.description: Returns all scans for the account.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

count.description: Total number of results for the requested service.

page.description: Current page within paginated list of results.

per_page.description: Number of results per page of results.

total_count.description: Total results available without any search parameters.

total_pages.description: The number of total pages in the entire result set.

allOf.description: A BOLA vulnerability scan report, versioned for future evolution.
Version 1 of the BOLA vulnerability scan report.
Overall report summary.
A verdict. `ok` means the scan passed, `warning` means the scan detected issues, `inconclusive` means errors prevented the scanner from reaching an accurate verdict.

verdict.description: Overall verdict of the vulnerability scan.

summary.description: Summary of all steps and findings.

tests.description: List of tests that were run.

items.description: Result of a single test.
Error that occurred during a test.

error_code.description: Numeric error code identifying the class of error, if available.

items.description: A single step in a test.

assertions.description: Assertions that were made against the received response.

items.description: Assertion that was made against the received response.

description.description: Human-readable description of the assertion, explaining what was checked.

allOf.description: The kind of assertion to make.

oneOf.description: Assert that an HTTP status code is within a range, e.g. to assert success for the 2xx range, or expected failure for the 4xx/5xx range.

max.description: Maximum (inclusive) status code of the range.

min.description: Minimum (inclusive) status code of the range.

observed.description: Observed value on which the assertion was made.

allOf.description: Outcome of an assertion. `ok` means the assertion passed, `fail` means the assertion failed, `inconclusive` means the scanner could not evaluate the assertion.

errors.description: Errors the step encountered that may explain absent or incomplete fields.

items.description: Error that occurred during a test.

error_code.description: Numeric error code identifying the class of error, if available.

allOf.description: HTTP request that was made.
Credential set that was used.
Identifies the role a request was made with. The credential set governs this role. `owner` is the resource owner, `attacker` attempts to access resources.

header_names.description: Names of headers that were sent.

allOf.description: HTTP method.

url.description: Exact and full URL (including host, query parameters) that was requested.

variable_captures.description: Variable captures requested for this step.

items.description: A variable to capture from the response body.

json_path.description: JSONPath expression used for capture, e.g. `"$.id"`.

request.description: HTTP request that was made, if any.

allOf.description: HTTP response that was received.
HTTP response body preview.

oneOf.description: Body received but unable to read as UTF-8. Raw bytes, base64-encoded.
Body received as valid UTF-8 text but not valid JSON.

header_names.description: Names of headers that were received.

status_text.description: HTTP status text, if available for the status code.

response.description: HTTP response that was received, if any.

allOf.description: A verdict. `ok` means the scan passed, `warning` means the scan detected issues, `inconclusive` means errors prevented the scanner from reaching an accurate verdict.

report.description: Vulnerability report produced after the scan completes. The shape depends on the scan type. Present only for finished scans.

status.description: Current lifecycle status of the scan.

target_environment_id.description: The target environment this scan runs against.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

4XX.description: Create a new Scan Config failure.

POST /accounts/{account_id}/vuln_scanner.description:
Creates and starts a new vulnerability scan. The response may include
non-fatal warnings in the `messages` array.

schema.description:
Create a new vulnerability scan. The `scan_type` discriminator
selects the scan variant and its required context fields.

credential_sets.description:
Credential set references for a BOLA scan. The scanner uses the
`owner` credentials for legitimate requests and the `attacker`
credentials to attempt unauthorized access.

attacker.description: Credential set ID for the attacker.

owner.description: Credential set ID for the resource owner.

open_api.description:
OpenAPI schema definition for the API under test. The scanner
uses this to discover endpoints and construct requests.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.
